features.title: Migrate copied Claude Code prompt content into Codex configuration
features.user_visible_behavior: Codex has a clean AGENTS.md entrypoint, project .codex/config.toml, custom agent TOML files, and a global Codex template without old Claude migration files.
verification: Confirm AGENTS.md is rewritten as the Codex project entrypoint.
Confirm GLOBAL-AGENTS.md exists as the ~/.codex/AGENTS.md template.
Confirm .codex/config.toml parses as TOML and uses documented Codex keys.
Confirm .codex/agents/*.toml parse as TOML and define name, description, developer_instructions.
Confirm legacy CLAUDE files and old Claude directories are removed.
evidence: Python tomllib parsed all CodeX/.codex/agents/*.toml files and confirmed each defines name, description, and developer_instructions.
rg confirmed Codex entrypoint, global template, and .codex references exist.
Old Claude migration files were removed from CodeX: CLAUDE.md, GLOBAL-CLAUDE.md, README.md, .claude/, agents/, tasks/todo.md, and tasks/lessons.md.
features.notes: Migration and cleanup are scoped to the CodeX/ copy. Root Claude Code configuration is intentionally left unchanged.

features.title: Create Codex high-efficiency configuration guide
features.user_visible_behavior: CodeX includes a README that explains installation, file responsibilities, agent usage, and the minimal high-efficiency workflow.
verification: Confirm README explains AGENTS.md, GLOBAL-AGENTS.md, .codex/config.toml, .codex/agents, tasks, and memory.
Confirm TOML and JSON configuration files still parse after adding README.
evidence: README includes structure, install steps, agent table, workflow, file maintenance rules, and minimal setup.
Python tomllib/json validation passed after README creation.
features.notes: README is human-facing; active Codex behavior remains in AGENTS.md and .codex/.
